Proper initial sizing prevents infrastructure over-provisioning. Calculate your recommended database pool size based on CPU cores and application load—free and instant. Values are current through 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you up-to-date.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **Pool ≈ 2×cores + spindles**. Direct application with your input values. Recommended database pool size based on CPU cores and load.

explanation:
Technical calculators for IT: latency, storage, SLA uptime, cloud costs. Decisions that scale.

## How It's Calculated

Calculate your recommended database pool size based on CPU cores and application load. Free and instant.

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside reasonable bounds.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a guideline. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are current through 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What's the recommended PostgreSQL pool size formula?
faq.a: Approximately 2× CPU cores for typical OLTP workloads.

faq.q: Does adding more connections increase throughput?
faq.a: No. More connections don't equal higher throughput—database saturation is real.

faq.q: How does PgBouncer help with pool capacity?
faq.a: External poolers like PgBouncer multiply your effective connection capacity.

faq.q: What connection timeout settings should I use?
faq.a: Both connectTimeout and queryTimeout are essential for robust connections.

faq.q: How do I manage idle connections?
faq.a: Close idle connections to free up database resources and reduce memory overhead.
Yes, all our calculators are free with no registration required.

faq.q: Do you store my calculation data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser—we never send data to any server.
